title: Web App cannot connect on external network
body:
Currently, I had using podman to run a web app container. I also added the pory to the firewall. But external network cannot connect to the IP.

The image is working in docker and run in docker can connect to the web app

code to run container
$: podman run --name shell -dt --pod new:pod-shell shell:0.0.2


podman version:
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Tue Feb  9 05:19:06 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Hyper-V hosting REHEL8

comments.body:
Can you provide info on the pod? Did this work previously but break
recently?

I run as root. The network using the default.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45072247/117689450-5916d980-b1ec-11eb-9b28-fd6d67a6ea23.png)

yes
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45072247/117691433-76e53e00-b1ee-11eb-82de-97db9311874b.png)

I also tried port forwarding to create the container. But still cannot connect 
$: podman run --name shell -dt --pod new:pod-shell -p 7001:80 shell:0.0.2

For clarity, the `-p` line on a container in a pod does nothing - the port must be forwarded to the pod, not to individual containers within the pod. That said, I see that it's included in your inspect of the pod, so it should be working.

@mccv1r0 Any ideas here? The Podman side of things seems correct.

> But external network cannot connect to the IP.

Is the local node able to connect via port  7001?   e.g. does `nc -n -v 127.0.0.1 7001` connect?

Is the default policy for FORWARD chain ACCEPT?  

Is sysctl `net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1` ?

Yes. I can use curl to the ip:port.

$: nc -n -v 127.0.0.1 7001
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45072247/117742556-9c4a6a00-b237-11eb-92c7-8c612e37e57f.png)

$: iptables -L
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45072247/117742824-2561a100-b238-11eb-93b5-5d2eec50057b.png)


![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45072247/117746834-a40e0c80-b23f-11eb-835d-2542cebafd6c.png)

Hi, zhangguanzhang
i tried your solution but still cannot connect
If could connect after stop firewalld , I think you should add the MASQUERADE for firewalld-cmd

> Yes. I can use curl to the ip:port.

This is not a podman issue.

The default policy for the FORWARD chain needs to be ACCEPT, or some firewalld equivalent.

Linux is not allowing packets to arrive on one interface and be sent on another.

Thanks everyone support. I solved the problem.

solution:
$: yum install iptables-services
